name: nix-config
description: A personal Nix system configuration (nix-darwin + home-manager) for macOS that includes Claude Code plugins, skills, and hooks as part of the dotfiles. Includes a deep-research-team SKILL.md and several plugin integrations (ai-sdk, homebrew, tts, pua-unicode, cc-maintenance).

findings_summary.notes: This is primarily a personal Nix dotfile configuration rather than a standalone agent skill. It gained stars due to its well-crafted Claude Code plugin configurations. The Claude-specific content lives under configs/claude/ and is symlinked via Nix into ~/.claude/. Notable contents: a deep-research-team SKILL.md (multi-agent research orchestration), several plugin SKILL.md files (ai-sdk-docs, audit-permissions, homebrew new-cask, icon-lookup, TTS cancel/say/start/stop), and hooks. Hook analysis: format-nix.sh (PostToolUse on Edit/Write for .nix files) runs nixfmt, deadnix, and statix — entirely benign. Other hooks (notify-ghostty.sh, prefer-mcp-web-tools.sh, shellcheck-lint.sh) are straightforward utilities. Claude settings.json is well-structured. The ai-sdk-docs skill runs shell scripts (fetch-doc.sh, list-docs.sh, query-models.sh) to retrieve Vercel AI SDK docs — benign. The audit-permissions skill uses Python scripts to discover and clean up settings — no dangerous patterns. No hardcoded credentials, no obfuscation, no data exfiltration. MIT licensed. The primary risk is that this is a personal dotfile repo and its Claude config may evolve without versioning guarantees.
